one_liner: DE at $592.90 has DCF fair value of $206 — 65% overvalued — with MACD bearish (-2.516) and Grade C fundamentals; Jim Cramer is bullish which historically is a contrary indicator.

price_action.vol_rank: Well below average — very thin volume
price_action.intraday_story: DE opened above $600, sold off all day to close near the low at $592.90 — a bearish intraday pattern on thin volume. Below the Bollinger Band midpoint.

news_catalyst.headline: Jim Cramer: 'Deere Is Incredible and Not Done Going Higher' — Contrarian Warning Flag
news_catalyst.summary: Jim Cramer's bullish endorsement on Deere is widely treated as a contrarian indicator by seasoned traders. Deere is facing agricultural equipment demand headwinds as farm incomes have normalized and order books have been declining. The DCF shows 65% overvaluation.

technical.summary: DE is in a neutral range with bearish MACD. DCF at $206 (65% overvalued) makes this an avoid. MACD -2.516 confirms downward momentum. Earnings August 20.
technical.wyckoff_narrative: Neutral Wyckoff below midrange. MACD bearish. Avoid at 65% above DCF value.

valuation.summary: DCF $206 vs $592 — 65% overvalued. Agricultural equipment cycle is past peak. Avoid.

key_concerns: Ag cycle downturn — order books declining
fundamentals.summary: C grade with cyclical headwinds and 65% DCF overvaluation. Avoid.

risks: 65% DCF overvaluation is the primary risk
Ag cycle downturn could accelerate if commodity prices fall further
